growth_records.medium: Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), pH 7.1 (closed system, no exogenous carbon/energy source, following wash from R2B growth medium)
growth_records.cite: Shoemaker et al. Microbial population dynamics and evolutionary outcomes under extreme energy limitation. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2021). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2101691118. [PMC8379937]
growth_records.snippet: we calculated a mean generation time of (log2(Nmut)/1,000 d)−1 ... Taxon Pseudomonas sp. ATCC13985 ... T2 days 50
growth_records.notes: Table 1 reports T2 (mean time to cell division, i.e. doubling time) = 50 days for this taxon during the 1,000-d energy-limited closed-system incubation, derived by the authors from pooled-sequencing mutation-frequency data (bmin from fmax*N(1000), generations = log2(Nmut)). Calculated here: doubling_time_h = 50 d x 24 = 1200 h; growth_rate_per_h (mu) = ln2/1200 = 0.000578 h-1. Conditions (25C, dark, PBS pH 7.1, batch, no exogenous resources) from Materials and Methods 'Constructing Survival Curves'. Authors themselves describe this estimate as 'admittedly coarse'.

up.method: dFBA model prediction (consumed from YR343); no numeric flux reported in text

sec.method: dFBA model prediction; no numeric flux reported
dFBA model prediction; no numeric flux reported
dFBA model prediction; no numeric flux reported
dFBA community-model prediction; no numeric flux reported
dFBA community-model prediction; no numeric flux reported

dFBA model prediction; no numeric flux reported

growth_records.cite: Wang et al. Formation of a constructed microbial community in a nutrient-rich environment indicates bacterial interspecific competition. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00006-24. [PMC11019790]
growth_records.snippet: Pseudomonas sp. GM17 secretes xanthine (XAN), octadecanoic acid (ocdca), and putrescine that are consumed by Pantoea sp. YR343. In return, strain YR343 is proposed to excrete glycerol, which is consumed by strain GM17... GM17 is predicted t
growth_records.notes: Maximum specific growth rate (mu_max) was measured by CFU counting and qPCR but numeric values are only in Fig 1d (not in text/tables), so growth_rate/doubling_time cannot be curated. Secretion/uptake compounds are dFBA MODEL PREDICTIONS (not measured fluxes) and are reported without numeric rates; captured with rate=null and clearly labeled. R2A composition not stated (referenced). Conditions: 30C, 200 rpm shaking, batch monoculture, serial transfer every 48 h. dFBA model input 'max uptake rate 10 mmol/gDW/h' etc. are arbitrary sweep constraints, NOT physiological measurements, and were excluded.

growth_records.medium: Minimal medium with glucose (Monod parameter assay)

up.method: calculated qS = mu/Yxs = 1.0 / (3.1e7 cells/mg); glucose consumption itself measured by Sigma glucose kit

growth_records.cite: Ogundero et al. Developing Lotka-Volterra Based Models to Describe Bdellovibrio Predation in a Batch and Chemostat Experimental System. Environmental Microbiology Reports (2025). DOI:10.1111/1758-2229.70141. [PMC12229818]
growth_records.snippet: the intercept and the slope is obtained to determine the value of the maximum growth rate (1.0 h-1) and the half-saturation constant (0.18 mg/L)... Prey yield 3.1 x 10^7 cells/mg of glucose
growth_records.notes: Pseudomonas sp. (prey) grown in glucose-limited minimal medium, batch, 30C, 200 rpm, 16 h. EXPERIMENTAL (flow cytometry + Sigma glucose kit) Monod parameters from Table 1 / Results: maximum specific growth rate mu_max = 1.0 h-1; glucose half-saturation constant Ks = 0.18 mg/L; prey (biomass) yield Yxs = 3.1e7 cells per mg glucose. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/1.0 = 0.693 h (calculated). Specific glucose uptake qS = mu/Yxs = 1.0 h-1 / 3.1e7 cells/mg = 3.23e-8 mg glucose per cell per h (calculated; cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h because cell dry mass not reported). Note: paper reports Pseudomonas grew even at zero added glucose, attributed to casein hydrolysate / residual C+N in medium base (minimal medium base composition not fully specified beyond glucose). Preculture (not scored separately): overnight in LB broth at 30C. pH of this assay medium not stated (predator-prey buffer elsewhere pH 7.6).

growth_records.cite: Syed Adnan Hasan et al. Degradation kinetics and pathway of phenol by Pseudomonas and Bacillus species. Biotechnology & Biotechnological Equipment (2015). DOI:10.1080/13102818.2014.991638. [PMC4684072]
growth_records.snippet: the maximum specific growth rates were 0.26, 0.43 and 0.78 h-1, respectively ... specific growth rates started to decrease when phenol concentrations were above 220, 310 and 320 mg/L in cultures of IES-Ps-1, IES-S and IES-B
growth_records.notes: Observed maximum specific growth rate mu=0.43 h-1 (OD600 slope, NB, 35C, 125 rpm, batch). td=0.693/0.43=1.61 h. Inhibition onset >310 mg/L phenol. Model-fitted mu_max (Table 2): Monod 0.51, Luong-Levenspiel 0.63, Haldane-Andrew 0.8 h-1 (Ks 71/77/121 mg/L; Ki(Haldane) 696 mg/L; Sm(Luong) 2174 mg/L, n=1). Phenol degraded via ortho-cleavage (catechol -> cis,cis-muconate); complete degradation ~56-60 h in 700 mg/L bioreactor. No Yxs/gDW reported, so phenol uptake not convertible to mmol/gDW/h.
